title: RoleDelegation
description: Time-bounded delegation of a delegator User's role (or all roles) to a delegate User within the same tenant. Both delegator + delegate MUST share `tenant_id` — cross-tenant delegation is refused at RoleDelegationObserver::creating (see rules.json `same_tenant_delegation`). `role_id` is nullable: null = ALL of the delegator's active roles for the delegation window; non-null = one specific role_id from the extended spatie-permission `roles` table. Composite unique on `(tenant_id, delegator_id, delegate_id, role_id, starts_at) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL` guards against creating duplicate concurrent delegations of the same role between the same actor pair. Hot-path index on `(tenant_id, delegate_id, starts_at, ends_at)` serves the `IsCurrentlyDelegate($delegate)` query used by RBAC PermissionResolver + workflow/approvals approver selector.

x-eloquent.relations_note: delegator() belongsTo User via delegator_id; delegate() belongsTo User via delegate_id; role() belongsTo Role via role_id (nullable — null = all-roles delegation); revoker() belongsTo User via revoked_by (nullable). The `is_all_roles` accessor is a convenience: `role_id IS NULL`.

tenant_id.description: Delegator + delegate MUST share this tenant_id (enforced by observer). Cascade on tenant erasure — delegations are hard-deleted with their tenant.

application_id.description: Cascades through tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §2 — role_delegations is not one of the eight rows carrying application_id directly, but delegations must be application-scoped for cross-app tenant isolation. Restrict on delete so an application cannot be removed while active delegations reference it.

delegator_id.description: The User handing over. Restrict on delete so a User cannot be deleted while active delegations reference them (must revoke first).

delegate_id.description: The User receiving. Restrict on delete (same reason as delegator_id).

role_id.description: Optional specific role being delegated. NULL = ALL of delegator's active roles for the delegation window (OOF flow). Cascade on role delete — if a tenant admin deletes a custom role, any delegation scoped to that role dies with it. All-roles delegations survive because role_id IS NULL.

delegation_type.description: Semantic hint for UX + audit filtering. `oof` (out-of-office; the default; symmetric) / `handover` (longer-lived transition, e.g. Alice leaves the finance team) / `emergency` (immediate + bypass min_advance_notice_hours; requires `emergency_delegation` entitlement).

starts_at.description: When the delegation becomes effective. Must be >= now() at creation (enforced by valid_delegation_window rule + observer). For future-dated delegations, `ActivateDelegationsJob` transitions the row through the effective boundary.

ends_at.description: When the delegation ceases. Must be > starts_at AND <= starts_at + config('delegation.role_delegations.max_days', 90). Enforced by rule + observer.

revoked_at.description: Populated on early revocation. NULL for pending / active / expired states — the state machine derives from starts_at / ends_at / revoked_at trio.

revoked_by.description: Who fired the early revocation. Must be the delegator OR a user with `delegations.manage_any` (admin). Set null on User erasure so the row stays queryable (the audit trail preserves the id anyway via HasAudit).

reason.description: Required (min 20 chars) — see rules.json. Audited. When delegation_type=`emergency`, expected to include a rationale citing the emergency policy.

revoke_reason.description: Populated on early revocation. Distinct from `reason` (the original creation rationale).

metadata.description: From HasMetadata. Free-form platform-ops notes bag. Internal-tier — never stores PII.

indexes.name: role_delegations_active_pair_unique
indexes.predicate: revoked_at IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL
indexes.description: Guards against two overlapping active delegations of the same (delegator, delegate, role) starting at the same moment. Note: does NOT guard against overlapping windows with different starts_at — that's the `DelegationSelfOverlapDetected` observer path.

indexes.name: role_delegations_delegate_lookup_idx
indexes.description: Hot-path serving `IsCurrentlyDelegate($delegate)` — used by RBAC PermissionResolver + workflow/approvals approver selector on EVERY permission check + approver resolution.

indexes.name: role_delegations_delegator_lookup_idx
indexes.description: Reverse lookup — 'who is currently delegating from me' — powers the /me/delegations list.

indexes.name: role_delegations_role_idx
indexes.description: For workflow/approvals — resolve delegators of a specific role_id to find current delegates for approver resolution.

indexes.name: role_delegations_expiry_scan_idx
indexes.predicate: revoked_at IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL

indexes.name: role_delegations_activation_scan_idx
indexes.predicate: revoked_at IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL

indexes.name: role_delegations_retention_scan_idx
indexes.description: Powers the daily `PurgeRevokedDelegationsJob` — finds rows past the 730-day retention window.

is_pending.description: Computed. `starts_at > now() AND revoked_at IS NULL`.

is_active.description: Computed. `starts_at <= now() < ends_at AND revoked_at IS NULL`.

is_expired.description: Computed. `ends_at <= now() AND revoked_at IS NULL`.

is_all_roles.description: Computed. `role_id IS NULL` — the OOF flow that delegates every role of the delegator.

remaining_hours.description: Computed. `(ends_at - now()) / 3600` for active delegations; null for non-active states.
